policy.text:
September 2023 New York State Medicaid Update pg. 7

Pharmacy Coverage Expanded to Include Over the Counter
Naloxone
New York State (NYS) Medicaid now covers naloxone over-the-counter (OTC) when either prescribed by the
practitioner of a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed to a NYS Medicaid member in compliance with the
State-issued naloxone standing order. Information regarding the naloxone standing order can be found on
the NYS Department of Health (DOH) “Standing Order for Naloxone in Pharmacies” web page, located at:
https://www.health.ny.gov/diseases/aids/general/opioid_overdose_prevention/pharmacy_standing_order.ht
m#:~:text=All%20pharmacies%20in%20New%20York,and%20opioid%2Dbased%20pain%20killers. A
pharmacy, in compliance with NYS law and regulations, and NYS Medicaid policy, may submit a claim and
dispense naloxone OTC pursuant to a fiscal order or via a standing order.
policy.title: New York State Medicaid Update September 2023 Volume 39 Number 14
policy.authority: New York State Department of Health

policy.text:
When dispensing naloxone OTC
via a standing order, all the following must apply:
• a NYS Medicaid member had specifically requested the item on the date of service (DOS); or
• a pharmacist-initiated dispensing per a determination of need/risk on DOS for a NYS Medicaid
member; and
• a pharmacy submits one course of therapy (two doses) with no refills; and
• the drug item(s) are dispensed according to:
Food and Drug Administration (FDA) guidelines;
NYS laws, rules, and regulations; and
NYS Medicaid Policy.
Billing a Standing Order:
1. Enter a value of "5" in the Prescription Origin Code field 419-DJ to indicate pharmacy dispensing;
2.
policy.title: New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2
policy.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
Pharmacy
providers who receive a “no TPL reimbursement” must ensure that the claim was adjudicated within the TPL’s claim
process requirements. This includes submitting the claim under the appropriate coverage benefit, medical or
pharmacy, such as with the Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) or by the National Drug Code
(NDC). Additionally, pharmacies not contracted with the member’s TPL Plan may need to attempt an override with the
plan, or enroll with the plan, or advise the member and prescriber of the need to change dispensing to a network
pharmacy.
It is the responsibility of the prescriber to prescribe, according to the plan’s formulary, and to pursue any claim issues
such as, but not limited to:
• Necessary prior authorizations or appeals, or
• Prescription alternatives (for a non-formulary or non-preferred drug) or
• Necessary changes (quantity or day supply, etc.) or
• Selecting an in-network pharmacy that is agreeable to the member, or
• Billing the appropriate benefit (medical vs. pharmacy or HCPCS vs NDC).
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
These orders must be
kept on file at the pharmacy.
• If billing for both the drug and administration, these claims must be submitted separately.
• For the drug, the claim must be submitted using the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field. The
pharmacy will be reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug Code (NDC) using
reimbursement methodology.
• For the administration, the claim must be submitted using procedure code 96372, and the pharmacy’s NPI
in the Prescriber ID field. A pharmacy will be reimbursed the current administration fee.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
All prescription drugs are reimbursed on a fee-for-service basis. Residential health care
facilities may:
• Operate an institutional pharmacy to provide these items or
• Contract with Medicaid enrolled pharmacies to provide these items to Medicaid members. The pharmacy
must be reimbursed by the facility for all non-prescription drugs and medical/surgical supplies. Prescription
drugs may be billed directly to Medicaid by the dispensing pharmacy on a fee-for-service basis.
Drugs billed directly to Medicaid are limited to prescription drugs included on the New York State Medicaid List of
Reimbursable Drugs: https://www.emedny.org/info/formfile.aspx and are subject to refill, frequency, quantity and
duration, step therapy, and prior authorization/approval requirements as described in this Manual.

Medicaid members with both Medicare and Medicaid (dual eligible Medicaid members) who have met their residency
requirements in a residential health care facility will receive their prescription drug coverage from their Medicare Part D
Plan.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
• The NYRx provides reimbursement for prescription drugs included on the NY Medicaid Pharmacy List of
Reimbursable Drugs, which can be found at: https://www.emedny.org/info/formfile.aspx.
• Prescriptions must be dispensed and billed by a Medicaid enrolled pharmacy, using the member’s individual
Medicaid Client Identification Number (CIN).
Smoking Cessation Policy
• Smoking cessation therapy consists of certain prescription and non-prescription agents.
• Some smoking cessation therapies may be used together. Professional judgment should be exercised when
dispensing multiple smoking cessation products.
• For all smoking cessation products, the member must have a prescription or fiscal order.
• NY Medicaid reimburses for over-the-counter nicotine patches included on the Medicaid Pharmacy List of
Reimbursable Drugs: https://www.emedny.org/info/formfile.aspx.
Emergency Contraception Drug Policy
Both prescription and OTC Emergency Contraception are a covered benefit for all Medicaid members without age
restriction. This includes individuals enrolled in the Family Planning Benefit Program.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

answers.headline_summary: Naloxone is reimbursed by NYS Medicaid when it is dispensed as an over-the-counter pharmacy benefit, either with a practitioner prescription or fiscal order or under the State-issued standing order. The pharmacy must submit a claim and follow the standing-order billing rules, including dispensing one course of therapy with two doses and no refills when billed under the standing order.

aligned_pairs.provider_manual: When a claim must be adjudicated under the correct benefit or billing method, providers must bill the appropriate benefit and follow the plan’s claim process requirements. The manual is broader than naloxone and does not give naloxone-specific standing-order instructions in this row.
aligned_pairs.policy_update: NYS Medicaid covers over-the-counter naloxone when prescribed by a practitioner or dispensed under the State-issued standing order. The pharmacy must submit a claim and dispense naloxone OTC pursuant to a fiscal order or standing order.

aligned_pairs.provider_manual: NY Medicaid reimburses prescription drugs fee-for-service through Medicaid-enrolled pharmacies using the member’s individual Medicaid Client Identification Number. Covered pharmacy drugs must be on the Medicaid Pharmacy List of Reimbursable Drugs.
aligned_pairs.policy_update: The policy is more specific for naloxone and says OTC naloxone is covered when prescribed or dispensed under the standing order. When dispensing under the standing order, the pharmacy must submit one course of therapy, which is two doses, with no refills.

similarities: Both sources require the pharmacy to use the correct billing pathway and submit a claim for reimbursement [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

Both sources tie reimbursement to Medicaid pharmacy billing rather than informal dispensing [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].
differences: The provider manual is broader and focuses on correct benefit selection and claim processing, while the policy update gives naloxone-specific OTC standing-order rules [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

The policy update adds the standing-order limit of one course of therapy with two doses and no refills, which the provider manual does not state in this row [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].
answers.caveats: The provider manual chunk used here is broader than naloxone and does not itself state the standing-order naloxone billing details.

answers.headline_summary: NYS Medicaid covers naloxone OTC when it is prescribed by a practitioner or dispensed under the State-issued naloxone standing order. The pharmacy must submit a claim and follow the required billing pathway, including the standing-order instruction to use Prescription Origin Code 5.

aligned_pairs.provider_manual: NYRx reimburses covered prescription drugs on the Medicaid Pharmacy List of Reimbursable Drugs. Prescriptions must be dispensed and billed by a Medicaid-enrolled pharmacy using the members individual Medicaid Client Identification Number. The manual states the general pharmacy billing rule, but it does not describe naloxone standing-order conditions in this chunk.
aligned_pairs.policy_update: When dispensing naloxone OTC via a standing order, the member must request it or the pharmacist must determine need or risk on the date of service. The pharmacy submits one course of therapy, which is two doses, with no refills. The item must also be dispensed according to federal and State requirements and the Medicaid policy.

similarities: Both sources require the pharmacy to follow a specific billing pathway before reimbursement is paid [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

Both sources tie reimbursement to the correct Medicaid pharmacy process rather than to an informal over-the-counter sale [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].
differences: The manual is broader and focuses on general Medicaid pharmacy billing rules, while the policy update gives naloxone-specific standing-order instructions, including Prescription Origin Code 5 [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

The policy update adds naloxone-specific conditions such as a member request or pharmacist determination of need or risk, one course of therapy, and no refills, which the manual chunk does not state [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

policy.text:
April 2024 New York State Medicaid Update pg. 2

Policy and Billing
For the drug, the claim must be submitted using the National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the ordering prescriber
in the Prescriber ID field. The pharmacy will be reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug
Code (NDC) using reimbursement methodology. For the administration, the claim must be submitted using
procedure code “96372” and the pharmacy NPI in the Prescriber ID field. A pharmacy will be reimbursed
the administration fee of $13.36.
Questions and Additional Information:
• Pharmacists should refer to the NYSED “Pharmacists” web page, located at: https://www.op.
nysed.gov/pharmacist, for additional information.
• NYRx claim questions should be directed to the eMedNY Call Center at (800) 343-9000.
• NYRx Pharmacy coverage and policy questions should be directed to the NYS Medicaid Pharmacy
Policy Unit by telephone at (518) 486-3209 or by email at NYRx@health.ny.gov.

provider_manual.text:
• Refills of compounds must be specifically requested by the patient or patient´s designee before the item is
prepared and submitted for payment.
• Ingredients in a compound claim will only be reimbursed if available on the NYS Medicaid List of Reimbursable
Drugs.
• Ingredients will only be reimbursed if they are compendia-supported for the intended route of administration
and indication.
• Compounds may not contain excluded drugs or be made for NYS Medicaid excluded indications as per the
Social Security Act §1927(d)(2), including, but not limited to drugs to treat weight loss or sexual dysfunction or
for cosmetic purposes.
• Compounds may not be made to bypass the criteria within the NYS Medicaid Fee-for-Service Programs.
• Compounds may not be made with or to replace drug products removed from the marketplace due to safety
reasons.
• Compounds may not be made in therapeutic amounts or combinations not FDA-approved, or compendia-
supported for use.
• Compounds may not be made to add coloring, flavoring, perfumes, or other non-active ingredient additives to
a commercially available product.

policy.text:
September 2023 New York State Medicaid Update pg. 7

Pharmacy Coverage Expanded to Include Over the Counter
Naloxone
New York State (NYS) Medicaid now covers naloxone over-the-counter (OTC) when either prescribed by the
practitioner of a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed to a NYS Medicaid member in compliance with the
State-issued naloxone standing order. Information regarding the naloxone standing order can be found on
the NYS Department of Health (DOH) “Standing Order for Naloxone in Pharmacies” web page, located at:
https://www.health.ny.gov/diseases/aids/general/opioid_overdose_prevention/pharmacy_standing_order.ht
m#:~:text=All%20pharmacies%20in%20New%20York,and%20opioid%2Dbased%20pain%20killers. A
pharmacy, in compliance with NYS law and regulations, and NYS Medicaid policy, may submit a claim and
dispense naloxone OTC pursuant to a fiscal order or via a standing order.

provider_manual.text:
These orders must be
kept on file at the pharmacy.
• If billing for both the drug and administration, these claims must be submitted separately.
• For the drug, the claim must be submitted using the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field. The
pharmacy will be reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug Code (NDC) using
reimbursement methodology.
• For the administration, the claim must be submitted using procedure code 96372, and the pharmacy’s NPI
in the Prescriber ID field. A pharmacy will be reimbursed the current administration fee.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
Multiple Source Drugs
Reimbursement is only available for those multiple source drugs contained on the List of Medicaid Reimbursable
Drugs.
For certain brand name prescriptions not in the Brand Less Than Generic Program, to be eligible for reimbursement,
prescribers must certify that the brand name drug is required by:
• writing directly on the face of the prescription “Brand Necessary” or “Brand Medically Necessary” in their own
handwriting in addition to the “DAW”, or
• in the case of electronic prescriptions, the prescriber must insert an electronic direction to clarify “Brand
Medically Necessary” or “Brand Necessary”.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
Pharmacy
providers who receive a “no TPL reimbursement” must ensure that the claim was adjudicated within the TPL’s claim
process requirements. This includes submitting the claim under the appropriate coverage benefit, medical or
pharmacy, such as with the Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) or by the National Drug Code
(NDC). Additionally, pharmacies not contracted with the member’s TPL Plan may need to attempt an override with the
plan, or enroll with the plan, or advise the member and prescriber of the need to change dispensing to a network
pharmacy.
It is the responsibility of the prescriber to prescribe, according to the plan’s formulary, and to pursue any claim issues
such as, but not limited to:
• Necessary prior authorizations or appeals, or
• Prescription alternatives (for a non-formulary or non-preferred drug) or
• Necessary changes (quantity or day supply, etc.) or
• Selecting an in-network pharmacy that is agreeable to the member, or
• Billing the appropriate benefit (medical vs. pharmacy or HCPCS vs NDC).
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
Note: Reimbursable drugs are listed alphabetically in sections by Rx Type.
Drug Coverage Limitations
Medicaid only provides reimbursement for drugs included on the New York State List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs
(unless provided by a facility which includes the cost of drugs in their all-inclusive rate). The following are examples of
drugs/drug uses which are not reimbursable by Medicaid in accordance with Policy and/or state or federal Legislation:
• Drugs used for the treatment of anorexia, weight loss or weight gain pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2).
• Drugs for the treatment of sexual dysfunction pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2), and Social Services Law §365-a(4)(f).
• Drugs without a federal rebate agreement pursuant to SSA §1927(a).
• Drugs indicated for cosmetic use or hair growth pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2).
• Drugs that are not approved as safe and effective by the FDA pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(4)(C).

provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

answers.headline_summary: Naloxone is reimbursed by NYS Medicaid when it is covered as an over-the-counter pharmacy benefit and dispensed under a valid prescription or the State-issued standing order. If the pharmacy bills both the drug and an administration service, the claims must be submitted separately.

aligned_pairs.provider_manual: The pharmacy is reimbursed for naloxone when the drug claim is billed using National Drug Code reimbursement methodology. The claim must use the ordering prescriber’s National Provider Identifier in the Prescriber Identification field. This is the current billing rule for the medication claim.
aligned_pairs.policy_update: NYS Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter when it is prescribed by the member’s practitioner or dispensed under the State-issued standing order. The pharmacy must submit a claim and dispense it pursuant to a fiscal order or standing order. This policy statement is broader because it describes coverage and dispensing conditions rather than the exact billing field.

similarities: Both sources say naloxone is reimbursable as an over-the-counter pharmacy benefit when the required order or standing order is in place [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

Both sources tie payment to a pharmacy claim for the drug rather than to informal dispensing alone [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].
differences: The provider manual gives the current billing method and requires the ordering prescriber’s National Provider Identifier in the Prescriber Identification field, while the policy update is broader and focuses on coverage and the need for a fiscal order or standing order [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

The provider manual also states that drug and administration claims must be submitted separately, but the policy update does not address administration billing [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

policy.text:
February 2024 New York State Medicaid Update pg. 4

Reminder: Pharmacy Coverage Includes Over the Counter Naloxone
New York State (NYS) Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter (OTC) when prescribed by the practitioner
of a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed to a NYS Medicaid member in compliance with the State-issued
naloxone standing order. Information regarding the naloxone standing order can be found in the NYS
Department of Health (DOH) Non-Patient Specific Prescription for Naloxone with Pharmacy Dispensing
Protocol document, located at: https://www.health.ny.gov/diseases/aids/general/opioid_overdose_prevention
/docs/naloxone_standing_order_pharmacies.pdf.
To comply with NYS laws, regulations, and NYS Medicaid policy, a pharmacy must submit a claim and
dispense naloxone OTC pursuant to a fiscal order or via a standing order.
policy.title: New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2
policy.authority: New York State Department of Health

policy.text:
When dispensing naloxone OTC
via a standing order all the following must apply:
• a NYS Medicaid member had specifically requested the item on the date of service; or
• a pharmacist initiated dispensing per a determination of need/risk on date of service for a NYS
Medicaid member; and
• a pharmacy submits one course of therapy (two doses) with no refills; and
• the drug item(s) are dispensed according to:
Food and Drug Administration (FDA) guidelines;
NYS laws, rules, and regulations; and
NYS Medicaid Policy.
Billing a Standing Order:
1. Enter a value of "5" in the Prescription Origin Code field 419-DJ to indicate pharmacy dispensing;
2.

policy.title: New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2
policy.authority: New York State Department of Health

policy.text:
April 2024 New York State Medicaid Update pg. 2

Policy and Billing
For the drug, the claim must be submitted using the National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the ordering prescriber
in the Prescriber ID field. The pharmacy will be reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug
Code (NDC) using reimbursement methodology. For the administration, the claim must be submitted using
procedure code “96372” and the pharmacy NPI in the Prescriber ID field. A pharmacy will be reimbursed
the administration fee of $13.36.
Questions and Additional Information:
• Pharmacists should refer to the NYSED “Pharmacists” web page, located at: https://www.op.
nysed.gov/pharmacist, for additional information.
• NYRx claim questions should be directed to the eMedNY Call Center at (800) 343-9000.
• NYRx Pharmacy coverage and policy questions should be directed to the NYS Medicaid Pharmacy
Policy Unit by telephone at (518) 486-3209 or by email at NYRx@health.ny.gov.

provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
All prescription drugs are reimbursed on a fee-for-service basis. Residential health care
facilities may:
• Operate an institutional pharmacy to provide these items or
• Contract with Medicaid enrolled pharmacies to provide these items to Medicaid members. The pharmacy
must be reimbursed by the facility for all non-prescription drugs and medical/surgical supplies. Prescription
drugs may be billed directly to Medicaid by the dispensing pharmacy on a fee-for-service basis.
Drugs billed directly to Medicaid are limited to prescription drugs included on the New York State Medicaid List of
Reimbursable Drugs: https://www.emedny.org/info/formfile.aspx and are subject to refill, frequency, quantity and
duration, step therapy, and prior authorization/approval requirements as described in this Manual.

Medicaid members with both Medicare and Medicaid (dual eligible Medicaid members) who have met their residency
requirements in a residential health care facility will receive their prescription drug coverage from their Medicare Part D
Plan.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
• Refills of compounds must be specifically requested by the patient or patient´s designee before the item is
prepared and submitted for payment.
• Ingredients in a compound claim will only be reimbursed if available on the NYS Medicaid List of Reimbursable
Drugs.
• Ingredients will only be reimbursed if they are compendia-supported for the intended route of administration
and indication.
• Compounds may not contain excluded drugs or be made for NYS Medicaid excluded indications as per the
Social Security Act §1927(d)(2), including, but not limited to drugs to treat weight loss or sexual dysfunction or
for cosmetic purposes.
• Compounds may not be made to bypass the criteria within the NYS Medicaid Fee-for-Service Programs.
• Compounds may not be made with or to replace drug products removed from the marketplace due to safety
reasons.
• Compounds may not be made in therapeutic amounts or combinations not FDA-approved, or compendia-
supported for use.
• Compounds may not be made to add coloring, flavoring, perfumes, or other non-active ingredient additives to
a commercially available product.

answers.headline_summary: Naloxone is covered by NYS Medicaid when prescribed by a practitioner or dispensed under the State-issued standing order. The drug is reimbursed through NDC billing, and the administration fee is reimbursed separately when applicable.

aligned_pairs.provider_manual: For the drug, the claim must use the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field. The pharmacy is reimbursed for the medication when it bills via National Drug Code using reimbursement methodology. This is the current billing rule for the medication claim.
aligned_pairs.policy_update: For naloxone, the drug claim must use the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field. The pharmacy is reimbursed for the medication when it bills via National Drug Code using reimbursement methodology. The policy also states the administration claim is separate when administration is billed.

aligned_pairs.provider_manual: The manual gives the broader claim-processing rule for pharmacy billing under the correct benefit. It says pharmacies must ensure the claim is adjudicated under the appropriate benefit and may need to use National Drug Code billing. This is broader than naloxone-specific coverage language.
aligned_pairs.policy_update: The policy says NYS Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter when prescribed by a practitioner or dispensed under the State-issued standing order. It also says the pharmacy must submit a claim and dispense naloxone OTC pursuant to a fiscal order or standing order. This is more specific than the manual’s broader benefit-billing rule.

similarities: Both sources say the naloxone drug claim is reimbursed through National Drug Code billing with the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update April 2024 Volume 40 Number 4].

Both sources treat the medication claim as separate from any administration claim when administration is billed [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update April 2024 Volume 40 Number 4].
differences: The provider manual is broader and focuses on correct benefit adjudication, while the policy specifically says naloxone is covered over the counter under a practitioner prescription or the State-issued standing order [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].

The policy adds the OTC and standing-order dispensing rule, while the manual does not state that naloxone-specific coverage condition [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines, New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2].
